We want to play something to score chips and mult. Highest base: Flush Five 160/16 but need 5 same suit. We have mostly diamonds: D_A, D_Q, D_T, D_7, D_6 are 5 diamonds. That's a flush of diamonds (Flush Five). But we need 5 cards, we have exactly 5 diamonds: indices 0,1,2,4,5. That is a Flush Five with level1: chips 160, mult 16. That's huge. Also we have two 5s (S_5, C_5) which are not used but can be included as extra discard if we play 5 cards? Wait we can play up to 5 cards. Flush Five requires exactly 5 cards. That would score a pair with two 5s but include 3 extra diamonds as junk: That would give pair chips 10+mult2 -> 20? Actually base chips 10, chips value 5+5=10 -> total 20 * mult2 = 40. Plus we have extra 3 cards (3 diamonds) that are unscored, but they get discarded, so we draw 3 new cards. But we might want to use the high flush. A Flush Five gives 160*16? Wait compute: Score = (Base Chips + Cards Value + Chip Additions) × (Base Multiplier + Mult Additions) × Mult Multipliers. For Flush Five: Base Chips 160, Base Multiplier 16. Cards Value sum of 5 cards values: D_A=11, D_Q=10, D_T=10, D_7=7, D_6=6 -> sum =44. So (44+160)=204. ×16=3264. That's huge. But we have 4 hands left, target 300. Even one hand is enough. We can play 5 diamond cards and win. But we need to play 5 cards exactly: the 5 diamond cards. We could also include the 5s but that would exceed 5 cards. So play indices 0,1,2,4,5 (D_A, D_Q, D_T, D_7, D_6). That yields score 3264 >300. That ends game, but we have to finish the round: We beat small blind, we get money, shop phase. But we must consider that we also have two 5s left in hand: S_5 and C_5. But those will be left in hand. That seems fine.

However, we must consider boss blinds may happen later. But early small blind, we can finish quickly.

Thus, best action: play those five diamond cards. No other action needed. So call play with cards [0,1,2,4,5]. Provide reasoning: flush five high score. Ensure we don't exceed 5. We'll win. Let's proceed.

message.reasoning:
We need to decide for the current pack opening. We have two spectral cards: Trance (Add a Blue Seal to 1 selected card) and Sigil (Convert all cards in hand to a single random suit). We have 8 cards in hand: indices 0 to 7 as listed. We also have current hand and available consumables etc. We have Jokers: Odd Todd (adds chips to odd ranks) and Half Joker (+20 Mult if hand <=3 cards). We also have Jupiter consumable that upgrades flush. We have no other consumables. We have 5 joker slots with 2 filled. We have 1 consumable slot used by Jupiter. We have 1 remaining consumable slot. So we can pick a consumable from the pack: Trance or Sigil. Both are spectral, immediate use.

We need to consider which is more valuable. Sigil converts all cards in hand to a single random suit. This would change all 8 cards to same suit randomly. That seems very disruptive, possibly good for flush synergy but random. Trance adds a blue seal to one selected card. Blue Seal: Creates the Planet Card for final played poker hand of round if held in hand (Must have room). We already have a Jupiter planet card in consumable slots. We have 1 consumable slot empty. If we apply blue seal to a card, we will use the slot to hold the planet card generated. But we already have Jupiter planet card; that is a consumable we already have. We can't have duplicate planet card? The planet card can be held in consumable slots; we have one empty. The planet card is a consumable that can be activated (blue seal). Blue Seal creates planet card for final played hand if held. So if we add blue seal to a card, that card will be used to create planet card when last hand played. We already have Jupiter, but that is level 1; maybe we can upgrade Jupiter with Planet Card? Wait, we have Jupiter consumable already. Planet card level up? Planet cards increase level of associated hand type. Jupiter level 1 means flush level increased by 1. We could upgrade it by using a planet card again? Actually planet cards are consumables that level up associated hand type when activated? They increase the hand type's level by 1. But we already have Jupiter at level 1. We could use a planet card again? The Jupiter planet card is consumed when used. But we can create another Jupiter planet card via a consumable card. The Planet Card itself is consumable that you hold. When you use Blue Seal on a card, it will create a planet card for the final hand of round. So we could add Blue Seal to a card, then at end of round create a new Jupiter planet card, giving us 2 Jupiter planet cards. But we have one slot currently used by existing Jupiter. If we have another Jupiter planet card, we would have two in consumable slots. But we only have 1 empty. So we could add blue seal to a card, use it at end to create Jupiter planet card. But we can also use it now? Wait, we only create a planet card at final played hand. So we won't have extra planet until after we play final hand of round. That may not be useful until next round. But we could use it to level up flush again if we play final hand this round as flush. But we might not be able to guarantee that final hand will be flush. But we could play flush at the end. If we play flush as final hand, we could get another level. That seems useful.

However, we also have a Sigil card which converts all cards to single random suit. That could create many flush opportunities or maybe straight flush. But random suit might not be good.

Which is better? Consider our current hand: A♠, K♥, K♣, Q♦, J♠, T♠, T♣, 3♣. We have many spades: A♠, J♠, T♠ (3 spades), plus K♥, K♣, Q♦, T♣, 3♣. We could attempt flush spades: We have 3 spades, but we could convert all to same suit? Sigil random suit could turn all to spades (maybe), but not guaranteed. If it's all same suit we can have flush. But we might also convert to hearts, clubs, diamonds. That seems random.

Trance: add Blue Seal to one card. That would be beneficial if we can ensure final hand is flush to use planet card to level flush again. We have Jupiter planet card already level 1; we could use it to level flush again to level 2. That would increase flush chips from 35 to 70? Wait base chips for flush level 1 is 35; plus Jupiter level 1 adds +15 chips. At level 2 flush would have 35 + 15*2 = 65? Actually planet card increases chips and mult for hand type by adding at level N: For level 1 flush: base chips 35, base mult 4. With Jupiter level 1: +15 chips, +2 mult. So flush level 2: base chips 35 + 15 = 50, base mult 4 + 2 = 6. So flush level 2: chips 50, mult 6. That's 300 points? Wait calculation: For flush with 5 spades: base chips 50 + card values (spade values). Suppose 5 spades high. Let's estimate: A♠=11, J♠=10, T♠=10, T♠? Wait T♠ already used? Actually T♠ is one spade, we have J♠, T♠, A♠. If we use flush of 5 spades: maybe A♠, J♠, T♠, Q? Not spade? We have only 3 spades. But we could convert other cards to spades via Sigil.

Sigil might convert all to same suit: if random suit is spades, we get 8 spades. We could play flush with 5 spades. That would give base chips 50, base mult 6, plus card values sum. The card values: Ace (11), Ten (10), Ten (10), Jack (10), Ten? Wait we have J♠, T♠, T♠ (two tens) but we only have one T♠? Actually we have T♠ and T♣. So if all to spades, we have two tens. We have J♠ and T♠, A♠, K♠? Not. We have K♠? We don't. We have K♥, K♣. So spades: A♠, J♠, T♠, plus maybe others after conversion. The rest of the cards would become spades as well, but we have 8 cards all spades. That could produce a straight flush? But we need consecutive ranks of same suit. We have Ace, King, Queen, Jack, Ten, Nine? We have 3♣, T♣, Q♦, etc. If all converted to spades, ranks become random? The conversion sets all to a random suit, but the ranks remain same. So we would have ranks: A, K, K, Q, J, T, T, 3. That's A, K, K, Q, J, T, T, 3. That may allow a flush, maybe a straight? We could play straight flush? We would have Ace-King-Queen-Jack-Ten? Thus, Trance is not as powerful unless we plan to use the new Jupiter planet card next round. But we also have one empty slot. But we already have Jupiter planet card level 1; we could use it next round anyway. But we might not have enough slots to hold both? We need to decide action. Current state: Phase SHOP, Round 6 Ante 3/8 Money $1. Jokers: Odd Todd and Half Joker. Consumable: Jupiter (level1). Shop items: Ancient Joker (cost 8), Walkie Talkie (cost 4). Pack: Arcana Pack (cost 4). Vouchers: Blank (cost 10). Reroll cost 5.

We have $1, can't buy anything. We could reroll but cost 5, not enough. We could buy voucher but cost 10, not enough. We could buy pack cost 4, not enough. So must leave shop. So next_round.

Thus we produce a tool call next_round.

We must provide reasoning.
